summary: Megan, Raj, and Brian convened on day three of an ongoing outage affecting the Detect product's event processing pipeline. Raj explained that a single point of failure in the event ingestion layer caused a cascading failure, leaving customers with zero threat monitoring visibility for approximately six hours. The remediation plan includes deploying redundant active-active processing nodes (live by end of day) and implementing a circuit breaker pattern (by Wednesday evening), with full tenant visibility restored in a phased rollout by tomorrow morning. The team aligned on communication protocols, retroactive security analysis, and a postmortem timeline.
actionItems: Megan Lawson: Draft updated customer communication explaining the phased rollout and revised timeline within the hour
Raj Kapoor: Send evening status update to Megan and Brian once the first phase of the redundant node rollout is complete
Raj Kapoor: Deliver internal retroactive event analysis summary report by Wednesday
Brian Cho: Prepare affected account breakdown with priority tiers and impact durations for the postmortem by Thursday

keyMoments.text: Brian reveals 112 open support tickets tied to the outage, with ~30 from priority 1 or 2 accounts and several vocal customers
keyMoments.speaker: Brian Cho

keyMoments.text: Raj confirms customers had zero threat monitoring data for six hours, with events queuing but not processing
keyMoments.speaker: Raj Kapoor

keyMoments.text: Megan raises the critical concern that no affirmative statements can be made to customers about security posture during the outage window
keyMoments.speaker: Megan Lawson

keyMoments.text: Raj pushes back on compressing the timeline, stating he would rather give a conservative estimate and hit it given the existing credibility issue
keyMoments.speaker: Raj Kapoor
